rag_rules: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes; use implementation scopes for assignments and subcomponent references.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use type-level guarantees for output dependencies.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reference only visible identifiers and declared helpers within the same annex scope.
Use only valid AADL features or auxiliary variables in AGREE expressions to prevent type resolution errors.
Ensure AGREE scalar type declarations use int, real, or bool; avoid package-qualified or undefined types.
When diagnostics reference unresolved identifiers, trace and fix the root cause or remove the invalid clause.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks, with type annexes for assumptions/guarantees and implementation annexes for assignments.
Assignments in implementation scope must target existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent ports or implementation-only paths in component type annexes.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use type-level guarantees for output dependency contracts.
